channel.title: Frontaliere Ticino — English
channel.description: News and guides for cross-border workers in Ticino

item.title: Checks in the Heart of Varese: Complaint and Expulsion
item.description: On the afternoon of March 23, extensive checks in the center of Varese led to a criminal complaint and the expulsion of a man. Details of the operations and the implications for Swiss border crossers.

item.title: Prevention for Men: The Beccaria Center is a Reference Point for All Ages
item.description: The Beccaria Center in Bellinzona offers urological prevention services for all ages.

item.title: Men and drones at work at night to control the fire in Laveno Mombello
item.description: The nighttime emergencies in Laveno Mombello require a rapid and coordinated intervention.

item.title: Border Crossings in Ticino: A Daily Reality
item.description: The Swiss canton of Ticino, located in the southern part of the country, shares a border with Italy. According to recent data, the number of people crossing the border daily is increasing, with a total of 12,500 individuals passing through the border in 2022. This trend is expected to continue in the coming years, with the canton's population projected to grow by 10% by 2030. 📊💡

item.title: Middle East conflict: billion-euro impact on energy in Ticino and Italy
item.description: Diesel exceeds 2 CHF/liter in Ticino after the attack on Iran. Energy price hikes for households and businesses: 5 billion per year in Switzerland.

item.title: Closures and diversions on A9: what to do during the storm
item.description: New week and already a storm of closures and diversions on A9

item.title: Lavizzara: issues with water network, no potable water in various areas
item.description: The water network in Lavizzara has been affected by issues, resulting in the lack of potable water in various areas

item.title: Gasoline prices in Switzerland: +10% to +22%
item.description: After a month of conflict, gasoline prices in Switzerland have risen between 10% and 22%

item.title: Locarno-Magadino via lake: either we change boat or we lose the service
item.description: The lake transport service between Locarno and Magadino is at risk of being suspended if a solution is not found to replace the current boat.

item.title: Stricter Controls for Cars Registered in Grisons
item.description: The Ticino intensifies controls on cars with Grisons license plates but parked in the canton, to counter tax evasion 📊💡⚠️.

item.title: 10 km of traffic jam at the San Gottardo: record disruptions for Easter Monday
item.description: Easter Monday return trip blocked: 10 km of traffic jam at the San Gottardo and 2 hours of delay. The Airolo bypass is closed.

item.title: In Como, il Tax Free continua a crescere grazie ai clienti ticinesi
item.description: Lo scorso anno un aumento del 6 per cento. Chi arriva dal Ticino lo fa soprattutto per acquisti nella grande distribuzione.

item.title: Long Hours of Waiting at the North Portal of the San Gottardo
item.description: Traffic heading south caused significant disruptions to the San Gottardo in the afternoon of Holy Thursday, with a 15-kilometer queue at the north portal and waiting times of up to two and a half hours.

item.title: Escape from Dubai, Ticino as an alternative?
item.description: The conflict in the Middle East is pushing entrepreneurs away from the Persian Gulf, in search of new destinations

item.title: Ukrainians in Ticino, the S Permit between aid and uncertainties
item.description: The S Permit has been granted to approximately 70,000 people in Switzerland, while the Ticino region hosts around 3,000 Ukrainian refugees

item.title: Monte Ceneri Tunnel Reopened: Traffic Returns to Normal
item.description: The Monte Ceneri Tunnel has been reopened following a temporary closure.

item.title: New Director for Bellinzona's Social Services
item.description: Federica Giudici has been appointed as the new director of Bellinzona's Social Services

item.title: Lavizzara: Non-Potable Water in Certain Areas
item.description: The Lavizzara Municipality has declared the water from Piano di Peccia non suitable for drinking until further notice, affecting several hamlets in the valley.

item.title: Protest Against Home Care Costs
item.description: Saturday, April 18, demonstration in Piazza in Bellinzona against the introduction of costs for home care.

item.title: In Bellinzona, the exchange of clothes unites sociality and sustainability
item.description: The initiative of the association Meteora promotes conviviality, solidarity, and environmental awareness.

item.title: Coop Recalls Cheeses: May Contain Salmonella
item.description: Coop has recalled some cheeses due to the presence of salmonella, which can be particularly hazardous for immunocompromised individuals, pregnant women, young girls and boys, and the elderly.

item.title: Trump: 'Intesa o sarà l'inferno'. Il giallo dell'ultimatum spostato a martedì
item.description: Trump annuncia un ultimatum alla Cina durante la Pasqua in Ticino

item.title: Varese: gets drunk, gets injured, injures a security guard. Treated and arrested
item.description: A man got drunk, got injured and injured a security guard in Varese.

item.title: Becoming a Pastor in Ticino
item.description: The Canton of Ticino offers a training program to become a pastor, open to anyone interested.

item.title: Course for Shepherds in Ticino
item.description: A new course for shepherds in Ticino, developed by the Unione Contadini Ticinesi, to meet the growing demand for qualified professionals.

item.title: The Monte Ceneri Tunnel has been closed due to technical issues
item.description: The Monte Ceneri Tunnel was closed a few minutes before noon on the A2 in the direction of Ticino.

item.title: 21 km of Traffic Jam on the A2 at San Gottardo: The Consequences for Ticino Cross-Border Workers
item.description: The A2 has become a mobile parking lot for cross-border workers heading to Switzerland. The 21 km traffic jam heading south at the San Gottardo pass is putting the patience of thousands of Italian-Swiss workers to the test.

item.title: Initiative on 10 million: vote on June 14, 2026
item.description: The Canton of Ticino at the center of the debate on the initiative aimed at limiting demographic growth in Switzerland.

item.title: Many farmers nearing retirement: the challenge of succession
item.description: Ticinese farmers nearing retirement: who is ready to pass on the torch?

item.title: Ticinese Municipalities Invest in Tourism Sector: Here Are the Details
item.description: Recent months have seen several Ticinese municipalities invest in tourist infrastructure.

item.title: The 'Fifth Switzerland' that has a weakness for Milan
item.description: Almost seven thousand Confederates live in the capital of the meneghino and in the municipalities of its province.

item.title: Nestle Opens Office in Lombardy and Offers 200 Job Positions: Here Are the Required Profiles
item.description: Nestle has announced the opening of a new office in Lombardy and offers 200 job positions: here are the required profiles

item.title: Car overturns on SP1 between Varese and Gavirate: two 20-year-olds injured, one in critical condition
item.description: Two young people were injured in the late evening of Saturday, April 4, when a car overturned on the SP1 between Varese and Gavirate. One of the two girls was airlifted to the San Gerardo hospital in Monza in red code.

item.title: Traffic Paralysed at San Gottardo: 'Does Anyone Know How to Get to Italy?'
item.description: The column of cars stuck at the north portal of the San Gottardo tunnel has reached 21 kilometres with peaks of over 3 hours and 30 minutes of waiting time 📊💡⚠️

item.title: Sunset in Cadenazzo: Vigilante Killed in Car Accident
item.description: A road accident in Cadenazzo has resulted in the death of a security guard

item.title: Alain de Raemy: 'The Meaning of Easter? A Message of Hope'
item.description: The apostolic administrator of the diocese of Lugano reflects on the world's sufferings and the meaning of the cross for young people.

item.title: Roveredo Reports: Permits Are Not an Accident
item.description: The Municipality of Roveredo opposes the version presented by the Graubünden government regarding the issuance of residence permits, highlighting risks and critical issues in inter-cantonal control.

item.title: Lavena Ponte Tresa: Child thrown off bicycle seat is in critical condition
item.description: A 2-year-old and 9-month-old child was rescued by an eliambulance in Lavena Ponte Tresa after being thrown off the bicycle seat.

item.title: SUPSI launches bachelor's in Sustainability in 2027: focus on Ticino
item.description: SUPSI will introduce a new bachelor's program in 'Sustainability and Systemic Transition' in September 2027, with enrollment opening on April 27, 2026 for the bridge year.

item.title: 88-Year-Old Woman Fends Off Pickpocket, Leads to Arrest
item.description: An 88-year-old woman defended herself against a pickpocketing attempt on the road in Muttenz, Switzerland, and had the perpetrator arrested.

item.title: Fuel discount: Italy extends the discount
item.description: The Meloni government extends the cut in excise duties until May 1st

item.title: Swiss man stopped with 900 packages of Pokémon figures
item.description: A 33-year-old man was stopped at customs with a shipment of 900 packages of Pokémon figures worth 6,900 euros.

item.title: Abolition of Property Value Tax from 2029
item.description: As of January 1, 2029, the property value tax will be abolished for private residences, as decided by the Federal Council.

item.title: Fuel Prices Too Low in Italy: Massive Exodus from Switzerland and Warning from Ticino Operators
item.description: The reduction of fuel taxes in Italy is causing an exodus of Ticinese motorists to the Italian border.

item.title: High Risk of Forest Fires in Ticino
item.description: The Canton of Ticino is on high alert due to the high risk of forest fires. The situation is critical due to the lack of precipitation and strong winds.

item.title: Car thieves flee with 40 keys and a Skoda in Ticino
item.description: Two teenagers broke into a Swiss garage and fled with a Skoda and 40 keys

item.title: Farewell to Salvatore Longo, the Luino border worker who died on the job in Cadenazzo
item.description: Salvatore Longo, 62 years old, died on the job while directing traffic in Cadenazzo.

item.title: Banks and Unions Agree on Wage Increase and Reduced Working Hours
item.description: The approved platform foresees a monthly wage increase of 518 euros and a reduction of the weekly working hours from 37 to 35 hours.

item.title: Border Guard Struck in Construction Site in Cadenazzo, Ticino
item.description: Salvatore Longo, a border guard from Luino, has passed away after being hit by a vehicle in a construction site in Cadenazzo, Ticino

item.title: Against the increase in public transport prices, let's implement our motion
item.description: The Greens are asking for a 25% reduction in the costs of the Arcobaleno subscription
